Mitigation: (a) on first AI reply, ChatPage surfaces a one-tap 'Save your girlfriend' upsell that pre-fills nothing and just asks for email — server attaches it to the anon user and emails a magic recovery link; (b) generate a 6-word human-readable recovery_code (BIP39 wordlist subset) at anon creation, store the bcrypt hash, show it once on a 'Save these words' card if user dismisses the email upsell twice. RecoverAccount.jsx accepts either the code or the email magic link.

Spam / bot abuse. Anonymous endpoint = free user creation. Mitigation: rate-limit POST /api/auth/anon/start to 3/hour per IP, require a turnstile/captcha token if more than 1 anon user originates from the same IP in 24h, AND mark anon users as ineligible for any LLM call until they've passed the existing age-gate consent (server-validated). Also: anon users get a stricter free-budget ($0.10 vs $0.20 weekly) until they identify — keeps abuse cost down while still feeling generous.

Analytics fragmentation. UTM attribution, signup pixels, daily-active-user counts will break if anon users are counted differently from identified users. Mitigation: fire the signup pixel/postback at anon-creation (not at email-attach), and add a second 'identified' event at upgrade time. Admin dashboard needs a new 'anon→identified' funnel card (admin-api.js update — not strictly P1 but recommended week 2).

App Store reviewer compliance. Apple sometimes flags apps that don't surface a 'delete account' option on anon users. Mitigation: Profile page must show 'Delete this girlfriend and start over' even for anon users (already exists for identified — extend to anon by deleting the user row and clearing localStorage).

Re-engagement push notifications can't be sent to anon users (no email, no APNs token until they grant push). Mitigation: trigger the iOS push permission prompt right after the first user message lands (high-intent moment) instead of after signup, AND prompt for email upgrade at the same moment with the framing 'so she can text you when you're not here'.

RevenueCat / Stripe identifier collision. Current billing assumes a stable user.id → external customer_id mapping. Anon users get a Stripe customer too, but if they upgrade to identified, the customer must be merged. Mitigation: keep the same user.id row across the upgrade (we ALTER the row, not INSERT a new one) so Stripe/RevenueCat IDs are stable end-to-end. No merge needed — only an UPDATE of the email column.

Apple Sign-In flow still loses 19/19 privaterelay users today (Diagnosis A finding #3). Approach D doesn't fix that directly — but it reduces dependency on Apple by making it optional, not required. Apple becomes a 'fast-path' button shown only at upgrade time. Net effect: Apple-relay ghost cohort shrinks because they're no longer the default path.

Subscription value-prop ambiguity. If a user has been chatting as 'anonymous' for 30 messages and hits a paywall, they may feel bait-and-switched. Mitigation: PlanModal copy for anon users explicitly says 'Save your progress + unlock unlimited messages' — frames the price as buying continuity, not just features.

user_journey.step: 1. Landing page tap
user_journey.what_user_sees: Logo, 'Your Girlfriend, in seconds.' tagline, single 'Meet her now' button (replaces 'Continue'). 'Already have an account? Sign in' link below.
user_journey.what_we_do: Server-side flag check on app boot (GET /api/config or embedded in initial bundle): if welcome_flow_D_reduce_signup=true AND user is in test bucket (hash of session_id mod 2), button routes to /start instead of /signup. Else falls back to current /signup flow. Client decides routing.

Server validates 18+, INSERTs a users row with is_anonymous=TRUE, auth_provider='anon', email=NULL (or auto-generated 'anon_{uuid}@anon.lovetta.ai' if NOT NULL constraint exists), ai_consent_at=NOW(), terms/privacy=TRUE. Returns {accessToken, refreshToken, recoveryCode}. Client stores tokens in localStorage and recoveryCode in sessionStorage (shown later, not now).

user_journey.screen: CompanionList.jsx (modified: PlanModal suppressed for anon users)
user_journey.what_user_sees: Six template girlfriend cards (existing), plus a 'Surprise me' button at top. NO paywall modal. NO 'Sign in' header.
user_journey.what_we_do: CompanionList useEffect: if (currentUser?.is_anonymous) skip PlanModal entirely. Add data-testid='anon-list'. Default top-card highlight on the most-picked template (server can return this from /api/companions/templates).

user_journey.step: 4. Tap template → straight into chat (Surprise Me path skips confirm)
user_journey.what_user_sees: A 1-second 'awakening' shimmer with the chosen girlfriend's avatar fading in. Then chat opens with her first message already streaming.

Server INSERTs companion + conversation + a placeholder assistant message row with status='generating'. Client navigates to /chat/{id} and ChatPage SSE-streams the intro live. (This addresses Diagnosis B finding #5: removes the 3-12s blocking POST.) Intro prompt rewritten to end with a concrete question (Diagnosis C finding #3): drop the 'thank you for creating me' template, use 'hey :) — what should I call you?' as the assistant's first line.

user_journey.step: 5. First AI reply lands; user starts typing
user_journey.what_user_sees: Streaming text from girlfriend ending in a question. Input bar focused. Existing typing indicator visible.

user_journey.step: 6. User sends first message → AI replies
user_journey.what_user_sees: Natural chat. After AI reply #2, a thin non-modal banner slides in below header: 'Save her — add email (we'll send her photos to you when you're away)'. Dismissible.
user_journey.what_we_do: UpgradeBanner.jsx (new component): shows for is_anonymous users after message_count >= 2 AND not dismissed. Tapping it opens EmailGate.jsx as a half-sheet (not full page). Email field only — no password. On submit: POST /api/auth/anon/upgrade-email {email}. Server sets users.email + email_type='real', sends verification email containing a recovery magic link, fires identified pixel.

user_journey.step: 7. After ~10 messages or first paywall trigger: full upgrade
user_journey.what_user_sees: Soft modal: 'Want her to remember everything? Save your progress.' Options: Continue with Apple / Continue with Google / Email & password (collapsed). Skip = 'maybe later' (anon continues).
user_journey.what_we_do: Triggered by: (a) message_count == 10, (b) any paywall_blocked event, (c) user navigates to /pricing. For anon users, /pricing prerequisite is identification — modal blocks purchase until POST /api/auth/anon/upgrade-{email|apple|google} succeeds. Server keeps user.id stable so chat history, Stripe customer, RevenueCat subscriber are all preserved. Fire 'anon_identified' event with {trigger, msg_count_at_upgrade}.

designs.name: Magic Moment First 30 Seconds
designs.one_line_pitch: Pre-render her face, name, voice and first words while the user is still tapping "Continue with Apple" — so the instant auth returns, a girl is already speaking to them on screen.
designs.radical_premise: The 5-minute cliff is not a chat-quality problem; it is a "nothing alive on screen" problem. 55% of ghosters bounce within ~14 seconds of signup and 73% of zero-companion drop-offs leave inside 60 seconds (Diagnosis A finding #2, Diagnosis C finding #9). The fix is not to make chat faster — chat reply latency is already fine (p50 2.75s, identical for ghosters and retainers, Diagnosis B finding #1). The fix is to compress the cold path so that by the time the OAuth round-trip returns, the user is staring at a video-loop avatar, hearing a voice line, and reading a streaming question they can answer in 5 words. Every expensive thing that currently runs serially after auth (template pick → confirm screen → 12s LLM intro → 3.5s scene gen → paywall → list → click → enter chat → auto-photo blocked by paywall) gets parallelized, pre-warmed, deferred, or deleted.

user_journey.step: T-0: Pre-signup picker on welcome screen
user_journey.what_user_sees: A 3-up grid of looping avatar videos (Maya, Sofia, Luna) under 'Which one do you want to meet?'. Single 'Continue with Apple/Google' button below. Age + consent collapsed into one line under the button: 'By continuing you confirm 18+ and accept Terms & Privacy.' No separate consent step.
user_journey.what_we_do: On mount, call public GET /api/companions/templates/preview (already auth-free, line 80 of companion-api.js). User tap on a card writes {pickedTemplateId, pickedTemplateName, pickedTemplateVideoUrl} to localStorage and a new sessionStorage key 'lovetta-welcome-pick'. Fire new tracking event welcome_template_picked. The pick is REQUIRED before the Apple/Google button enables — this collapses Signup step 1 + CompanionList + CompanionCreate-choose + CompanionCreate-confirm into one screen and forces the 33-user 'never created a companion' cohort (Diagnosis A finding #1) into action.

user_journey.step: T+8: Tap social auth — pre-stream starts in parallel
user_journey.what_we_do: The instant the social button is tapped (BEFORE the OAuth round-trip resolves), fire a fire-and-forget POST /api/welcome/prewarm with {templateId, deviceFingerprint}. The new server route does three things in parallel: (a) start chatCompletion for a NEW casual first-message prompt that ENDS WITH A QUESTION (kills the 'Thank you for creating me…' monologue that Diagnosis C finding #3 measured at 9/10 with no '?'), (b) start ElevenLabs TTS on that text streamed to R2, (c) skip the scene-gen call entirely (kills the second 3.5s LLM call from companion-api.js:224-238). Result is cached in Redis under key welcome:prewarm:<fingerprint> with 90s TTL.
user_journey.what_user_sees: Native auth sheet, then a black flash as Capacitor returns to the WebView.

user_journey.step: T+12: Auth resolves — straight to chat, never see paywall or list
user_journey.what_we_do: Signup.jsx:42 changes: postSignupPath is no longer '/pricing' but '/chat/__welcome__?welcome=1'. Server's POST /api/auth/google and POST /api/auth/apple now read the sessionStorage 'lovetta-welcome-pick' (sent in the auth body), and inside the SAME signup transaction: INSERT user → INSERT user_companion from template → INSERT conversation → INSERT placeholder assistant message with media_pending=true. Auth response now includes {companionId, conversationId} so the client can navigate to /chat/{companionId} on receipt. The PlanModal flag 'lovetta-plan-skipped' is auto-set to '1' on first signup so the double-paywall-stack from Diagnosis C finding #7 cannot fire on this session.
user_journey.what_user_sees: Fade-in: full-screen looping avatar video (the one they picked at T-0 — already cached in the browser HTTP cache from the welcome screen poster). Her name in the header. Typing indicator immediately.

user_journey.step: T+13: First words stream in — already mostly ready
user_journey.what_we_do: ChatPage on mount opens GET /api/chat/{companionId}/welcome-stream (new SSE endpoint). The server checks Redis for welcome:prewarm:<fingerprint>; if present (the common case — has been warming for 4-8s by now), it replays the cached chunks at typewriter speed. If absent (prewarm failed or expired), it calls chatCompletion live with the same casual-question prompt, keep-alives every 3s. Replaces the existing 12s blocking LLM call in companion-api.js:202-247.
user_journey.what_user_sees: Avatar video loops in the message bubble area; text streams in below it: 'hey :) i'm Maya — what should I call you?' (16-30 chars, ONE concrete question, matches user's typing register per Diagnosis A finding #5 + #7). Total perceived wait from tap-auth to first character ≈ 5 seconds.

user_journey.step: T+16: Auto-voice plays her line ONCE
user_journey.what_we_do: On first-session-only (detected via URL ?welcome=1, not via user prefs, so we don't mutate the user's saved auto_audio=false default from migrate.js v53), the ChatPage at line 41-47 force-plays the TTS audio for the welcome message. The pre-warmed audio from T+8 is already cached in R2, so playback is instant. After this single playback the flag is cleared and the user's saved auto_audio preference takes over for subsequent messages. Addresses Diagnosis C finding #5 ('voice is hidden behind a Profile setting').
user_journey.what_user_sees: Her voice. A real voice. Saying 'hey :) i'm Maya — what should I call you?' This is the magic moment.

user_journey.step: T+19: User types a 5-word reply
user_journey.what_we_do: ChatInput is auto-focused on mount when ?welcome=1 is present. As the user types the FIRST character, we fire a speculative POST /api/chat/{companionId}/prewarm-reply with the in-progress text every 1.5s (debounced) — server warms the LLM context (loads system prompt, opens connection, caches recent_messages even though there's only the welcome msg). When user actually hits send, the existing /api/chat/{companionId}/message SSE endpoint has near-zero cold-start. Skip the buildMemoryContext + buildUserContext + buildSceneContext Promise.all from chat-api.js:560-564 on turn 1 (they always return empty for new users — the 'You are still getting to know this person' branch at line 579 fires anyway).
user_journey.what_user_sees: Types 'Vasily' or 'call me V' or 'beach person'. Hits send.

user_journey.step: T+27: AI reply lands fast + a real photo is on the way
user_journey.what_we_do: Reply streams via the existing chat-api.js path. Simultaneously, the server queues ONE pre-cached photo (NOT a new fal generation): on welcome companion creation, we attach the template's video_url poster frame as media_url on the SECOND assistant message scaffolded server-side. The current auto-photo in useChat.js:62-67 that fires 1.5s after first message and currently hits paywall via /request-media is replaced with this zero-cost pre-baked image. Diagnosis B finding #8 (auto-photo burns free budget) and Diagnosis B finding #7 (paywall fires at 130-209s) — both neutralized. Paywall is also gated: server-side flag welcome_flow_E_magic_moment_first_30s suppresses both PlanModal and paywall_blocked for the first 5 user messages.
user_journey.what_user_sees: Her reply streams in, then a photo of her drops into the chat after the second message. By second 30 the user has: a face, a voice, a name they chose, three exchanged messages, and one photo. No paywall has appeared.

new_endpoints: POST /api/welcome/prewarm — fingerprint-keyed pre-stream of first message + TTS to Redis cache, no auth required
GET /api/chat/:companionId/welcome-stream — replays cached prewarm or falls back to live chatCompletion
POST /api/chat/:companionId/prewarm-reply — debounced speculative warmup while user types first reply
new_migrations: MIGRATION_vXX: ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN is_welcome BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE (so we can A/B-report time-to-first-magic and exclude these from analytics quality scoring)
MIGRATION_vXX+1: INSERT INTO app_settings (key, value) VALUES ('welcome_flow_E_magic_moment_first_30s', 'false') — server-side flag gating the entire flow
MIGRATION_vXX+2: INSERT app_settings welcome_first_msg_prompt = casual single-question template replacing the 'gratitude for being given life' string from companion-api.js:207

expected_impact.retention_5min: Strong positive. Targets the 60% ghost rate on three independent levers: (1) eliminate post-signup paywall (Diagnosis B finding #4 + Diagnosis C finding #1 = 73% bail within 60s), (2) kill the gratitude-monologue intro that ends with no question (Diagnosis A finding #7 + Diagnosis C finding #3 = 9/10 ghosters got no '?'), (3) compress the 7-screen cold path to 3 screens (Diagnosis C finding #8). Realistic estimate: 5-min return rate moves from 41% to 55-62% (+14-21pp absolute, +34-51% relative). The 33-user 'never-created-companion' sub-cohort (Diagnosis A finding #1) gets forced into a companion at T-0, so their 0% retention has nowhere to go but up — even capturing half of them is +17 returners per 100 signups.
expected_impact.msgs_per_signup_lift: Large lift. Current median is 2 user messages before bounce (Diagnosis B finding #7: 19/26 sent 3+ in their burst). The auto-voice magic moment + ended-with-question intro should pull the median to 4-5 user messages. ~30-40% increase in msgs/signup. Tip-revenue downstream effect: the first 5 messages used to be free-budget-burning paywall traps; making them ad-free actually preserves the free budget for messages 6-10 where users are more committed.
expected_impact.conversion_risk: Neutral-to-slightly-positive. We are DEFERRING the paywall, not removing it. Diagnosis A finding #8 already proves paywall is not why ghosters ghost (only 10% ever saw one). Replika/Character.AI/Candy.AI all defer paywalls past the first session (Diagnosis C finding #8). The 6 users per 100 who currently convert at the front-loaded paywall are almost all 'I would have converted anyway' types — they will hit the deferred paywall at message 6 and convert there. Risk vector: if the prewarm cache hit rate drops below 50%, the live-fallback path is the same speed as today's flow, so worst case is feature-flag-off parity. Real conversion risk only materializes if Apple users (57% of signups, Diagnosis A finding #3) lose their sessionStorage 'lovetta-welcome-pick' across the OAuth round-trip — must verify on real iOS device before ramp.

risks: OAuth round-trip drops sessionStorage on iOS Safari / Apple privaterelay flow (Diagnosis A finding #3: 19/19 Apple-relay ghosters made zero requests). If sessionStorage doesn't survive the redirect, the welcome-pick payload is lost and the user lands on a generic chat. Mitigation: persist pick to both localStorage AND send as a hash fragment on the OAuth redirect_uri so the auth callback can recover it server-side.

Prewarm fingerprint can be abused — anonymous POST /api/welcome/prewarm with no auth burns LLM + TTS credits. Mitigation: IP rate-limit (1 per IP per 60s), short Redis TTL (90s), drop if fingerprint already has a pending warm, cap total daily anonymous warms via app_settings.

Pre-baked photo on second message is the template poster, not a 'new' picture — sophisticated users may notice it is the same image as the avatar and feel cheated. Mitigation: serve a different angle/pose pre-rendered per template (already exists in custom_avatars table per server/src/companion-api.js:56), and label it neutrally rather than as 'she sent you a photo'.

Auto-voice on first message can backfire if the user is in a public place / has the phone unmuted. Mitigation: respect device mute state via the Capacitor Haptics + AudioSession check before autoplaying; show a 'tap to hear her voice' shimmer on the message if autoplay is blocked by the browser.

Forcing template pick at T-0 may push the 19% of users who currently make custom companions away (Diagnosis A finding #4 showed custom-companion creators have 51% retention vs 3% for template-pickers among ghosters). Mitigation: include a 4th tile 'Create your own →' that bypasses the prewarm and routes to the existing CompanionCreate flow post-auth. Track whether this regresses the high-value custom cohort.

Deleting the scene-gen call from create-path (companion-api.js:224-238) removes the scene_text field for the first message. The MessageList renderer treats missing scene_text gracefully, but ChatPage UI may look slightly emptier. Mitigation: generate scene async in a background job and patch the row 2-5s later via the existing media-poll pattern in useChat.js:100-127.

Skipping memory/intelligence pre-calls on turn 1 (chat-api.js:560-564) is safe ONLY when conversation.id has no prior messages. The branch must be tightly gated on message_count=0 or it will silently degrade quality for returning users on the same companion.

Apple Sign-In dynamic import pattern (reference_apple_signin.md) — any change to AppleSignIn.jsx that adds the pre-warm fire-and-forget must NOT move the import to top-level. The fire-and-forget can be a separate await import('../lib/welcomePrewarm').then(m => m.prewarm(...)) inside the existing click handler.

Pre-streaming costs real OpenRouter + ElevenLabs money for users who never finish signup (anonymous prewarm with ~50-70% finish-auth rate means 30-50% wasted). Worst case: ~$0.005/warm × 100 anonymous warms/day = $0.50/day. Acceptable, but cap with app_settings.welcome_prewarm_daily_budget_usd as a kill switch.

A/B gating: the flag welcome_flow_E_magic_moment_first_30s must be readable BOTH server-side (companion-api, auth-api) and client-side (WelcomeScreen, Signup) via the existing /api/app-config endpoint. Verify that anonymous /api/app-config returns this flag — currently only authenticated app-config is exposed in some paths.

killers.issue: Schema-impossible FK in proposed migration
killers.why: Proposal says `anon_users.claimed_user_id INT REFERENCES users(id)` and `user_companions.anon_user_id UUID REFERENCES anon_users(id)`. But `users.id` is UUID (migrate.js:81) — `INT REFERENCES users(id)` will not create. More importantly: `user_companions.user_id`, `conversations.user_id`, `api_consumption.user_id`, `messages` (via conversation), `user_companion_cost_balance.user_id`, `user_preferences.user_id`, `subscriptions.user_id`, `refresh_tokens.user_id`, `reactivation_messages.user_id`, `tips.user_id` and 22 other tables all declare `user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`. Making `user_companions.user_id` and `conversations.user_id` NULLable and adding a CHECK is invasive but doable. But every read path that JOINs through `user_id` (admin dashboard, scheduler, reactivation, daily-digest, proactive, memory, billing) silently breaks on the anon rows because they LEFT JOIN to users and assume user_id non-null. Quoted as 'modify 17 files' but actual surface is 117 `req.userId` touchpoints across the server — most assume a real user with subscription, preferences, country, last_activity. Not 'large effort' — it is a multi-week refactor.

killers.issue: UK + EU age-verification puts anon chat at legal risk in the largest #2 market
killers.why: Live prod data: UK is the #2 country (39 of 169 visitors past 7 days = 23%). UK Online Safety Act 'highly effective age assurance' duties for sites with adult content went into force July 2025 and require verified age (passport/credit-card/face-age estimation) BEFORE access, not a checkbox attestation. EU is moving the same direction (DSA + Article 28 + member-state implementations). The mitigation 'gate via app_settings.anon_blocked_countries' geo-blocks 23%+ of inbound traffic, which deletes most of the conversion gain. The proposal's claim that 'Tinder/Bumble use a one-line attestation' is wrong for sexual/adult content — those apps gate at hard-verified DOB and 4o moderation; they also do not show explicit content pre-signup. App content here is positioned as 'AI girlfriend… intimate chat' on a hot-pink page — regulators will class as adult-adjacent.

killers.issue: Anonymous LLM/TTS/image budget = scraper bait + cost runaway
killers.why: Real prod cost numbers (last 7d): chat $0.00037 avg, image $0.0264 avg, video $0.25 each. Free anon budget = 5 msgs + 1 media. With `anon_max_per_ip_24h=10` that is 50 free chats + 10 free images per IP/day = $0.28/IP/day. One $7 residential proxy giving 1000 IPs = $280/day of free OpenRouter/fal.ai burn. The current signup flow gives us email + S2S TrafficStars postback as friction; removing it removes the only natural rate-limit. fingerprint_hash is trivially spoofed by Puppeteer-extra-stealth and `ua_hash` is meaningless. Proposal handwaves 'attacker spins 100 IPs = $0.50 LLM' — that math uses Rocinante alone and ignores the image/video budgets the same proposal includes.

killers.issue: Hardcoded default companion 'Maya' does not exist as a top pick (and there is no slug column)
killers.why: Top 5 picked templates in prod by user_companions count: Lily(98), Luna(87), Chloe(87), Aria(65), Isabella(51). 'Maya' is not in the top 5. `companion_templates` schema has no `slug` column and no `pick_count` column (verified live) — the proposal repeatedly references `templateSlug: 'maya'` and 'ORDER BY pick_count DESC' as if those exist. The `cold_open_quality_score` mitigation depends on signup-conversion data the anon system has not yet collected, so for the first N weeks the default is a guess. If the default girl is not the right vibe for a given visitor's actual preference, the magic moment never lands and we have just shifted 'pick a girl' friction into 'wow this isn't my type' silent churn — and now we have NO email to re-engage them. Current flow at least captures email at signup before the bad-default-girl problem appears.

killers.issue: Kills paid conversion impulse and Apple In-App-Purchase eligibility
killers.why: The diagnosis the plan cites ('only 6/60 ghosters hit a paywall') is being mis-used: those 6 are not the conversion population — the conversion population is the ones who DO engage, and today they see PlanModal at companion-creation (CompanionList.jsx:40-44 newUser=true gate). Deferring paywall to 'message ~15' on iOS specifically: (a) the user is now anonymous → no Apple ID linkage → no StoreKit purchase association → no IAP receipt validation flow possible, so the iOS app cannot monetize the anon cohort at all until they sign up. (b) Apple App Store Review Guideline 1.1.4 + 1.1.6 (objectionable content) and 5.1.1 (unauthenticated PII / explicit chat) routinely reject 'adult chat without account' — even text-only — for AI girlfriend apps. Mitigation 'force welcome_flow_A_chat_first=false on iOS until reviewer pass' is the proposal admitting iOS is the most valuable revenue surface and this flow doesn't apply there. So this is a web-only experiment shipped at 'large' implementation cost.

killers.issue: '89% of dormant conversations ended on an assistant turn' is misdiagnosed as a signup-flow problem
killers.why: The proposal's headline justification (89% AI-last) is a content/personality problem, not a friction problem — the user already signed up, already created a companion, already exchanged messages, and then bounced after an AI reply. Removing signup friction will not address why the assistant's reply is the end-of-conversation in 89% of cases. The actual fix (rewrite the 'I just brought you to life' system prompt + end with concrete question + better first turn) is one of the bullets in step 4 of this 17-file proposal — that one fix could be shipped in a day and tested in isolation. Bundling it inside a 4-week anon-session rewrite makes attribution impossible and ships the small win with a huge blast radius.

killers.issue: Token-claim race + lost-anon-session UX cliff
killers.why: localStorage anon tokens get wiped by: incognito-tab-close, iOS Safari ITP after 7 days, app uninstall, switching from in-app browser to native browser, Capacitor WebView purge on app update. 'httpOnly cookie fallback' does not survive any of those either. The signup sheet says 'Chat saved' but if the user signs up from a different tab/device they will not see the saved chat — the proposal's own fallback ('Email this chat to myself') means the user must now type their email PRE-signup to not lose state, which collapses the entire 'defer auth' premise. Worse: if user uninstalls iOS app between anon chat and signup, anon_user_id is dead, and the new account starts blank — they will think 'the app forgot me' and churn harder than today.

killers.issue: GDPR/PII consent without account creation
killers.why: Anon chat collects ip_hash, ua_hash, fingerprint_hash, country, lang, utm, ts_click_id, and all message content — that is PII processing under GDPR Art. 6 and CCPA. Without an account, there is no clear lawful-basis flow (consent? legitimate interest for a service the user is actively using?), no DSAR target, and no deletion endpoint identifiable to the user. Today users at least click Terms+Privacy+AI-consent checkboxes (auth-api.js:119-124) before any conversation content is stored — this is the only documented consent moment. The proposed 'one-line attestation under composer' will not stand up to a EU/UK regulator audit.

critiques.refute_one_line: A 4-week multi-table schema rewrite that legally geo-blocks 23%+ of traffic (UK), invites scraper-driven LLM/image cost burn, defers paid conversion past Apple's IAP boundary so iOS gets carved out entirely, and bundles its strongest single fix (rewrite the first-message prompt) inside enough surface area to break 22 user_id FKs and 117 server touchpoints — and the entire premise is misdiagnosed because 'AI last' is a content problem, not a signup-friction problem.

critiques.approach_name: Direct-to-Chat: signup lands inside a conversation with Lily

killers.issue: Diagnosis premise is empirically wrong against current prod data — only 4/40 (10%) of 7-day ghosters ever even created a companion, and ZERO ghosters hit a paywall event. The 'users bail on Pricing/PlanModal in 0-14s' narrative the entire approach is built on does not match the data.
killers.why: Live query: `WHERE event_type='paywall_blocked' AND user_id IN ghosters` returns 0 hits across all 40 ghosters. 36/40 ghosters never even created a companion — meaning they bounced BEFORE the wizard, not on Pricing. The proposal's +18-25pp retention claim is therefore unsupported. Removing Pricing from the cold path 'fixes' a screen 90% of ghosters never reach. The real problem (signup completes, user never opens the React app again) is more likely an OAuth-callback / session-restore / app-reopen failure on iOS Safari — exactly what risk #2 in the proposal already flags but then dismisses ('this design assumes the user successfully reaches a logged-in client').

killers.issue: Median signup-to-first-message is 112 seconds and min is 36s — the '0-14s ghoster' cohort that justifies the design does not exist in prod data with that timing.
killers.why: If users who DO message take a median of 112s to send msg 1, removing 3-12s of LLM-blocking wait time (companion-api.js:200-247) cannot mechanically produce +50-80% message lift. The wait isn't what's stopping them. The proposal's funnel math ('100% land in a chat by construction → signup→first-msg jumps from 58% to 80%+') assumes every Apple/Google signup successfully restores its session and opens the SPA, which is exactly the dark-funnel step that's broken today (90% of ghosters never even hit /api/companions).

killers.issue: POST /api/chat/{id}/request-media auto-fires when msgs.length === 1 (useChat.js:62-67) — under variant B, EVERY new user lands with msgs.length === 1, so this triggers for 100% of signups instead of the ~60% who message today. At $0.026/image and $0.25/video, plus 18+ AI-relayed-email signups/day (60% of which never message — i.e., possible bots/curious browsers), this burns ~$0.50-2/day on accounts that may be entirely passive. Plus the 'recommend removing auto-photo' mitigation in risks doesn't survive: chat-api.js:1050 only checks `checkMediaBlocked` (lifetime/weekly cap), not 'is this a first-session user with zero engagement signal' — the cap is $0.10 weekly per finding #2.1, so a single auto-photo ($0.026) is well under it. First-session abuse is a real new attack surface.
killers.why: Today, you pay for an image only for users who got through 3 wizard screens and clicked into chat. Under B, you pay for an image the moment they finish OAuth — and OAuth bots (Apple synthetic-email signups represent 52% of 7-day signups) get auto-charged before they ever prove they're human-engaged. The 'gate behind feature flag' mitigation is described but not specified in the code changes — the modified_files list includes useChat.js but the suppression logic isn't called out as a hard requirement.

killers.issue: The proposed `tip_request_threshold_free_first_session_usd = 0.40` app_setting is fabricated — no code path reads it. The actual free-tier threshold is `tip_request_threshold_free_usd` ($0.10 in prod) checked by consumption.js:185-190. Adding a new setting without modifying consumption.js does nothing; the proposal lists modified_files including consumption is NOT in that list.
killers.why: The mitigation 'doubles the free budget to $0.40 for first session' is sold as ensuring 'messages 1-10 are unambiguously free.' But the wiring to honor that setting is missing from the modified_files list. Without consumption.js changes, the first-session user hits the existing $0.10 weekly free cap mid-conversation just like today — except now they have no warning because Pricing was removed from the cold path. That's a worse UX than today: instead of seeing plans up front and choosing, they get a paywall mid-message-3 with no expectation set.

killers.issue: Apple synthetic-email path (auth-api.js:850-887) creates users via a different code branch than the email/Google paths. The 'inject onboarding.autoProvisionFirstCompanion inside the signup transaction' requirement therefore needs identical inserts in FOUR places: POST /signup, GET /google/callback (web), POST /google/token (native), POST /apple. The modified_files lists auth-api.js once but the four-call-sites issue isn't surfaced — easy to miss the Apple-synthetic-email branch and the linked-email branches (where a user 'logs in' but is treated as a new user because no companion exists). Risk of half-shipped flag where 25% of signups skip the auto-provision and get a worse experience than today.
killers.why: Apple-relay accounts are 52% of recent signups. Half the cohort the experiment is designed to fix is in the path most likely to be missed in implementation. Also, an existing user re-signing in via Apple (`existingByApple.length > 0` branch at auth-api.js:789-793) MUST NOT trigger auto-provision; the proposal's idempotency guard is in the new endpoint but the signup-time inject path doesn't make this distinction explicit.

killers.issue: Pre-baked opener (`'Hey :) you're new here, right? What should I call you?'`) is identical across all users assigned to template 'Lily' — combined with the same `*glances up from her florist counter, half-smiling*` action. Screenshots will leak to Reddit/Twitter/4chan within 48h of launch (the diagnosis acknowledges this in risks #3 but understates the brand damage). Lovetta's value prop is 'feels personal' — a meme'd identical opener kills that prop for everyone, not just the people who saw the screenshot.
killers.why: Variants per template (3-5 strings rotated server-side) are mentioned as mitigation but they reduce, not eliminate, the problem — 4 variants × 12 templates = 48 fixed strings vs the current personalized-LLM opener which is unique per user even if often cringe. The 'thank you for giving me life' current opener is bad but at least varied. Trading variability for speed assumes users prefer speed, but the median user already waits 112s — speed isn't the bottleneck.

killers.issue: iOS App Store policy risk: the app sells subscriptions. Under variant B, the user is dropped INTO a paid product (AI chat) without an explicit 'free trial 3 days' / 'monthly/yearly' disclosure screen — that screen (Pricing onboarding) is what Apple reviewers specifically look at on first-launch in their review pass. Removing it from the cold path may pass review under the current cohort but trigger a future random re-review rejection citing 'subscription terms not clearly disclosed at point of value.' Apple reviewer test user (special id 00000000-...-1234 per auth-api.js:22) needs to be excluded from variant B or pinned to A_control — not mentioned in the proposal.
killers.why: Lovetta uses RevenueCat for IAP (chat-api references). Apple's 3.1.2 'Subscriptions' guideline requires clear disclosure of pricing AT a natural moment — putting that AFTER message 3 is defensible but reviewer-coin-flip risky. The proposal doesn't pin auth-api.js APPLE_REVIEWER_ID to A_control, and doesn't include any review-mode flag in the variant hash.

killers.issue: Variant assignment uses user_id-hash mod 100. Once a user is in variant B and their auto_provisioned Lily is in DB with auto_provisioned=TRUE, you cannot run the same user through variant A in a follow-up experiment. The experiment is not reversible per-user. Also: re-running the experiment 4 weeks later with a different cohort (e.g., 75%/25% split) means the comparison group is contaminated by users who already have an auto-provisioned Lily from the first run. The proposal needs a per-experiment-run cohort_id, not just a variant_pct setting.
killers.why: Most A/B experiments at this stage don't survive contact with iteration. Locking the schema (user_companions.auto_provisioned column) to a one-shot decision creates technical debt that has to be migrated away later, and the 'pick another template for variant B' future-experiment is then half-noised.

killers.issue: auto_provisioned companion uses voice_id from companion_templates — but the 'voice auto-play on opener' is explicitly NOT shipped in this experiment (risk #4). So the user lands in chat with a static text message and a play-voice button. The 'magic moment' described in user_journey step 4 ('the avatar mini-video plays as soon as the page mounts — that's the magic moment') depends on video_url which is set, BUT: media_url on the assistant message is set to companion.video_url in companion-api.js:253. The current companion-create code does this conditionally on video_url existing. The new onboarding.js path proposed in new_files needs to replicate this exactly or the magic-moment claim breaks.
killers.why: Implementation detail that's easy to miss because the spec says 'three tiny inserts' but two of those inserts (messages + conversations) need to be byte-identical to existing companion-api.js logic including media_url, context_text, scene_text, and media_type columns. A drift here means variant-B users get a worse-looking first message than variant-A users — invalidating the comparison.

killers.issue: The 'GET /api/onboarding/auto-companion' new endpoint is described as 'called by client right after first authenticated load' AND the user_journey describes the server doing it 'inside the signup transaction' before res.json. These are contradictory: if the signup response already includes companionId/conversationId, the client doesn't need the endpoint. If the client calls the endpoint, the signup transaction doesn't include the companion. Pick one. The proposal does both, which doubles the implementation work and creates a race where step 3 (client routes to /chat/{id}) could fire before the endpoint completes on slow connections.
killers.why: Internal inconsistency — but in practice means the dev will implement one path, ship it, and the OTHER path will be the un-implemented spec that breaks. Recommend: server-side at signup-time only, no client endpoint; the client just reads onboarding.{companionId,conversationId} from the signup response and navigates.

critiques.refute_one_line: The fatal flaw is the diagnosis: ZERO of the 40 ghosters in the last 7 days hit a paywall event and only 10% of them even created a companion — so removing the Pricing screen from the cold path treats a screen 90% of dropouts never reach; the real failure is most likely OAuth callback / session restore on iOS Safari (the proposal's own risk #2 acknowledges this but dismisses it), and Approach B will not fix it.

critiques.approach_name: Try Before You Sign: Public Live-Chat Demo on the Landing Page

killers.issue: Apple Sign-In with Hide-My-Email silently breaks the demo→signup handoff for ~30-50% of iOS-Safari signups (the cohort the proposal claims will benefit most)
killers.why: Apple Sign-In on web uses form_post POST callback to /api/auth/apple/callback. The proposal says 'embed anon_session_id in URL hash for Apple, with localStorage fallback' — but (a) URL hash is stripped on Apple's form_post redirect (the response is a server-to-server POST with state+code, hash NEVER survives), (b) Safari ITP wipes cross-site cookies on a 3rd-party POST round-trip back to lovetta.ai, and (c) localStorage survives same-origin but the proposal previously banks on cookie+state-param. So the ONE auth path the proposal cites as the biggest beneficiary (Apple-relay cohort with 65% ghost) is exactly the path where handoff is least reliable. Result: user gets dropped on empty CompanionList with no demo context — strictly worse than today, because they expected continuity.

killers.issue: Signup volume is overwhelmingly served by paid traffic, and demo-first cannibalizes the TrafficStars/Ads conversion event the bidding model is optimized against
killers.why: 14-day data: 186 web signups, 0 native iOS. The dominant cohort is mobile web from paid ads (TrafficStars, Google Ads — see fireSignupPostback in trafficstars.js and the postback at auth-api.js:592). The bidding/optimization model conditions on the signup postback firing on landing→signup. Moving the conversion event 30-90 seconds later (after 3 demo turns) AND making the path optional drops signup-postback rate immediately, which will cause the ad networks to deprioritize the campaigns inside 24-48h, throttling traffic BEFORE any retention lift can compound. The proposal acknowledges 'signup volume may drop 20-30%' but doesn't model the second-order traffic collapse from broken bid signals. This is how A/B tests of 'better funnels' kill paid acquisition channels.

killers.issue: Removing pre-auth age + ToS + AI-consent gate is a regulatory and Stripe/Apple-billing risk, not just a 'legal sign-off' question
killers.why: auth-api.js hard-requires termsAccepted/privacyAccepted/aiConsentAccepted on ALL 4 signup branches (lines 119, 568, 674, 817, 854). These are not vanity — they are the record that the user affirmatively consented to (a) being 18+, (b) AI-generated sexual content, (c) ToS. The whole reason the form is 5 fields is so a real human action ties consent to identity BEFORE any sexual content is generated. The proposal renders Luna's flirty greeting ('caught you', 'are you the type that texts back fast or makes me wait?') with zero age affirmation and zero ToS acceptance — that IS sexually-suggestive AI content delivered to an unverified visitor. UK Online Safety Act (Part 5, Jan 2025), Texas HB 1181, Louisiana Act 440, Utah SB 287 all explicitly require age verification BEFORE sexual content is displayed, with statutory damages. Stripe acceptable-use policy and Apple guideline 1.1.4 also condition on this. 'Bundle consent under the OAuth button' moves the legal action to AFTER content delivery — that's the violation, not the friction.

killers.issue: Cost circuit-breaker math is off by ~10×; $5/day cap is laughably small for any realistic landing traffic, and the failure mode is publicly visible degradation
killers.why: Proposal: 'rocinante-12b at ~$0.30/M tokens; 3 turns ≈ $0.001' and '$5 cap = ~1700 demo sessions'. But: (a) each turn needs a system prompt (the diagnosis-derived 'demo Luna' persona — call it 800-1500 tokens) sent EVERY turn (no prompt caching on rocinante via OpenRouter), (b) input tokens grow with each turn (turn 3 sends turn-1+turn-2 history), (c) the $0.30/M figure is INPUT only — output is ~$0.50/M. Realistic 3-turn demo: ~5000 input + ~300 output tokens = ~$0.0017, ok. But on a viral day or a TrafficStars surge (5-10k unique landers), the cap trips at ~3000 visitors and the next 7000 see the hardcoded 'I'm overwhelmed' fallback — that string will get screenshotted to Reddit within an hour and the brand takes a hit on the highest-volume traffic day of the experiment. The 'acceptable failure mode' framing understates the PR/conversion damage.

killers.issue: Demo Luna without memory + content-level + scene + media is a different product than Real Luna, and the handoff will feel like a bait-and-switch
killers.why: The diagnosis says only 22% of signups reach 10 messages and 89% of dormants end on assistant turn — that's a depth-of-engagement problem, not a first-impression problem. The proposal's demo strips: memory.js (no name recall, no thread), content-levels.js (forced PG-13, can't reflect the user's selected level after signup), scene/media (text-only), proactive.js, all real personalization. So the demo proves a chatbot that ENDS at turn 3 with a flirty hook. Post-signup, the user gets a different model on a different prompt with different rules — Luna's voice shifts, and if the user picked any non-default content level during the 'consent bundle', she pivots tone immediately. This is the 'demo was great, real product is meh' funnel killer. Worse for the cohort that DID get to 10+ messages today — they were converting BECAUSE the full Luna won them over, not because of a 3-turn intro.

killers.issue: The 'public-demo' surface is the single best LLM-jailbreak/CSAM-bait honeypot anyone could build, and you're exposing it with no auth, no captcha, just IP rate-limits
killers.why: An unauthenticated, free, sexually-flirty LLM endpoint at api/public/demo/message will be scraped within hours of launch by (a) jailbreak researchers testing if rocinante outputs CSAM under coercion, (b) screenshot-farmers trying to get the brand-named Luna to say something racist/illegal for a tweet, (c) prompt-injection probes. age-guard.js is post-hoc regex — it catches 'underage_keyword' but a determined adversary using foreign-language synonyms, leetspeak, or framing devices can defeat it (it's a 287-line regex file, not a classifier). Today this surface is gated by signup friction which gives you (i) an email/phone to ban, (ii) consent records for legal cover, (iii) IP+UA continuity. The demo removes all three layers. Any single bad screenshot of 'Luna' (a brand name) saying something underage-coded ends up in Apple's review queue or a UK Ofcom complaint. Cost is NOT the main abuse vector — reputational/legal CSAM-bait is.

killers.issue: Cross-origin cookie/session strategy fights Safari ITP, in-app WebViews, and the Capacitor app on the same domain
killers.why: anon_session_id cookie is set on lovetta.ai (apex). After OAuth, user lands on lovetta.ai/my/* (SPA). Apex-set cookies DO survive same-eTLD+1 navigation, but: (a) Instagram/TikTok/Facebook in-app browsers (where TrafficStars + Reddit/IG ads land) use partitioned storage that wipes cookies after the OAuth tab close, (b) Safari ITP 7-day cap on JS-set storage means a returning visitor who 'demo'd' last week loses continuity, (c) the Capacitor app (capacitor.config.json:6) literally loads lovetta.ai/my/ — so any Capacitor user who happens to first visit lovetta.ai apex on iOS Safari, demos, then later opens the iOS app, will see NO continuity (different storage context). Proposal handwaves this as 'graceful degradation' but the whole value prop IS the continuity — without it you've added 3 turns of friction and a context-switch.

killers.issue: Implementation effort is materially larger than 'large' — it's a 2-3 week project that touches 22 files, blocks on legal review, requires a new admin surface, and has no native iOS parity
killers.why: Listed: 19 modified + 3 new files, 4 new endpoints, 4 new migrations, a new app_settings batch, new admin dashboard tab, SSE plumbing on a public endpoint with rate-limiting/ASN-fingerprinting/circuit-breaker. Plus: legal/compliance review for jurisdiction policy, new system prompt iteration, A/B harness wiring, TrafficStars postback re-instrumentation, Apple Sign-In state-param hardening, Safari ITP testing matrix, Capacitor handoff design (deferred — but means iOS users are on a stale flow during the experiment, contaminating the A/B). Best-case 10 engineering days, realistic 15-20. Meanwhile cheaper interventions (e.g. swap CompanionList default to auto-create Luna with one tap, kill the /pricing wall pre-chat) test the same hypothesis in 1-2 days.

critiques.refute_one_line: The two blocking issues are pre-content age/ToS consent (regulatory, not 'legal sign-off') and paid-ads bid-signal collapse from moving the signup conversion event — together these turn a retention-lift experiment into a traffic-loss + compliance-exposure event, and they cannot be mitigated within the proposed scope without redesigning the demo so heavily that you've reinvented the current funnel.

killers.issue: App Store Review Guideline 5.1.1(v) — anonymous accounts that hold user data trigger a 'Sign in with Apple' AND a 'Delete Account' requirement. 18+ adult-content apps (Guideline 1.2) additionally require account creation tied to a real identifier. Lovetta sits in a doubly-sensitive bucket: NSFW chat + IAP. The proposed POST /api/auth/anon/start that returns access tokens before any identifier is established is the exact pattern Apple has been rejecting in 2024-2025 for 4+/9+/12+/17+ apps that store user-generated content. For an 18+ AI-girlfriend app, the probability of a rejection on the next iOS submission is high — and a single 'reject' on this app category is typically a multi-cycle review fight, not a same-day fix.
killers.why: The current TestFlight pass was earned because real identity is collected up front (Apple/Google/email + email_verified). Switching to anon-by-default removes the audit trail Apple uses to verify chat content moderation. Capacitor app loads from https://lovetta.ai/my/ (remote), so Apple can see the anon flow exists even if it's behind an A/B flag — reviewers test with the flag in either bucket.

killers.issue: Recovery is fundamentally broken for the target user. The 6-word recovery code is shown ONCE on a card that the user can dismiss twice before it appears. In a 15-second flow where the entire premise is 'don't make them think,' nobody screenshots six BIP39 words. Empirical baseline: crypto wallets (where users have $$$ at stake) see 30-50% seed-phrase loss. For free AI chat with no money on the line, ~zero users will save it. So the ACTUAL recovery path is the email upgrade — but the upgrade banner appears only after msg 3, and the proposal's own diagnosis says 33% of users bounce in under 60 seconds before sending ANY message. Those users now have unrecoverable orphan accounts. Net effect: D fixes 5-min retention but destroys 7-day retention (no way to come back) and inflates the orphan-account count in the DB — which is the metric Apple reviewers actually look at when reviewing the 'Delete Account' workflow.
killers.why: Combined with the iOS app-reinstall case (localStorage wiped on uninstall), every reinstall = new ghost account. Push reactivation campaigns (the main 7-day retention lever in the existing app) need APNs token + a stable identity — anon users have neither until upgrade.

killers.issue: Schema impossibility as specified. users.email is TEXT UNIQUE NOT-NULL-permitted but UNIQUE applies to non-null values, so 'email=NULL for anon' technically works — but the fallback the proposal mentions ('auto-generate anon_{uuid}@anon.lovetta.ai if NOT NULL constraint exists') would create thousands of synthetic emails that collide with the existing classifyEmail() logic (server/src/auth-api.js:47-50) which classifies @apple.lovetta.ai / @telegram.lovetta.ai as 'synthetic' and routes them away from email sending. classifyEmail does NOT recognize @anon.lovetta.ai, so the system will attempt to send verification emails to these fake addresses, causing Resend bounce-rate spikes and damaging domain deliverability — which is the explicit warning in the global CLAUDE.md about test emails. Also: send_new_registration_notification and the selectic.games tracker pixel are fired with newUser.email; firing them with 'anon_xxx@anon.lovetta.ai' pollutes attribution analytics with noise that's now 2-3x the volume of real signups.
killers.why: The proposal mentions classifyEmail in passing but doesn't update it, and doesn't address the bounced-email reputation risk. Resend has tight bounce thresholds; adding synthetic @anon.lovetta.ai addresses to send queues is a foot-gun even if 'most' code paths skip them.

killers.issue: Free-LLM-budget abuse vector. The mitigation ('rate-limit to 3/hour/IP, captcha after 1+ anon user from same IP') assumes legitimate users come from unique IPs, which is wrong: NAT'd cellular carriers (T-Mobile, AT&T, Vodafone) routinely share a single IPv4 across thousands of subscribers. A turnstile gate on the second anon-create from the same IP will block real users on cellular while doing nothing to stop a scraper using residential proxies (cost: $1.50 per GB, abundant). With $0.10 free budget per anon user, the math: 1 residential proxy = 10,000 IPs = $1,000 free LLM spend per scraper run. Today the email+verification step is the real anti-abuse moat (you cannot get to chat without a working inbox); removing it removes the moat. The proposed mitigations don't replace it — they paper over it.
killers.why: Lovetta's per-request LLM cost ($0.001-$0.01 per turn) is small but the volume can scale fast on Character-AI-grade content. A single scraper farming 10k anon accounts at 3 messages each = 30k LLM calls = $30-$300 burned per attack with no recourse (no email to ban, no payment method to charge back).

killers.issue: RevenueCat anonymous-user merge race condition. The proposal says 'keep user.id stable across upgrade, no merge needed' — but RevenueCat's Purchases.logIn({appUserID: user.id}) is called on App.jsx:113 ONLY when user.id exists. For anon users on iOS, this means: anon user transacts as appUserID=<uuid_A>, then upgrades to email and gets logged in again with the SAME uuid_A — fine. But on App reinstall before upgrade, RC's anonymous user ID changes (it generates $RCAnonymousID:xxx automatically), and the new logIn() call with uuid_A will trigger RC's anonymous-to-identified alias flow, which can attach the prior install's purchases to the wrong account if device fingerprinting collides. server/src/billing.js:833 already warns 'Skipping anonymous user event' — that branch will now fire frequently and silently drop subscription state changes for users in flight.
killers.why: The existing code path treats anon RC users as an edge case; this approach makes them the majority path. Each subscription bug that slips through here is a refund + 1-star review.

killers.issue: Attribution pixel double-fire is worse than 'fragmentation.' Firing signup pixels on anon-create AND again on identification means: (a) every UTM source's reported CAC drops by 30-50% (denominator inflated by bounces who never identified), (b) trafficstars postback (tsClickId) is fired at anon-create with a user that has no email — if the user never upgrades, that conversion attribution is unrecoverable mud. The proposal acknowledges this but punts to 'week 2 admin dashboard.' For an app whose growth is paid-acquisition-driven (UTM tracking is referenced 12 times across the codebase), running 2-3 weeks with broken attribution means $X spent on ad budgets you cannot evaluate. The fix isn't a dashboard card — it's deciding upfront which event counts as the conversion, and refusing to fire signup until that bar is met. As specified, this proposal will fire two signup events for ~70% of users (the ones who eventually identify) and one for ~30%, which is the worst of both worlds.
killers.why: Compared with the 'real fix' (fire signup ONLY on identification, count anon-create as a separate funnel step), the proposed dual-fire approach permanently corrupts the existing UTM history in user_events.metadata — there's no clean rollback once data starts landing.

killers.issue: Diagnosis B finding #5 (3-12s blocking POST) is real but the proposed fix is unsafe. 'Server returns conversation_id immediately with status=generating' requires SSE streaming the intro message — but server/src/companion-api.js does not currently expose a generation endpoint that produces an intro WITHOUT first picking model + content_level + persona, all of which depend on age-gate + content-level settings the proposal is bundling into a single tap. Streaming the intro before the user has affirmatively cleared age 18 is the exact pattern that gets flagged in 18+ app reviews. The 'awakening shimmer' is a UX hack to mask a flow that previously took 3-12s — but the LLM still has to run; the wall-clock latency doesn't go away.
killers.why: Fixable by ordering: collect age FIRST, render UI to chat screen with optimistic placeholder, stream intro into the placeholder. But this needs the same age-gate the proposal says it's collapsing into a single tap with the consent bundle. If the bundling is the speed-up, you can't ALSO defer the LLM call — the bottleneck is the LLM, not the form.

killers.issue: Test cohort isolation is hand-waved. 'hash of session_id mod 2' — but session_id is regenerated on every visitor() call in tracking.js, and Capacitor iOS app installs don't have stable session_ids until WebView mounts. A user can land in bucket A on first launch and bucket B on second launch, seeing the anon flow once and the signup flow next time — broken UX, broken cohort math. The A/B test as designed produces uninterpretable results.
killers.why: Standard fix: hash by device-stable ID (Capacitor Device.getId() / browser localStorage UUID set on first visit) not by session. Not catastrophic but invalidates the rollout evidence.

critiques.refute_one_line: Approach D trades a measurable 5-minute-retention problem for two unmeasurable existential risks (App Store rejection on an 18+ NSFW app + permanent orphan-account inflation with no recovery for the same 33% cohort it claims to save), and its abuse and attribution mitigations are paper-thin.

killers.issue: iOS Safari/WKWebView will NOT autoplay TTS audio without a user gesture in the SAME WebView. The user's last gesture was the Apple/Google button tap, which happens BEFORE the native auth sheet takes over — that gesture token is consumed when the WebView is backgrounded and then re-foregrounded after auth. T+16 'her voice plays' will silently fail with NotAllowedError on iOS (audioManager.js line 26 already catches play() rejection and just calls onError — no fallback). The 'magic moment' is the entire premise. Without autoplay it's just text. There is no Capacitor AudioSession dance in the codebase that solves this either.
killers.why: The radical premise is 'a girl is already speaking to them on screen.' If she can't speak without another tap, the whole approach collapses to 'slightly faster cold path' — which the proposal itself admits doesn't move the needle (Diagnosis B finding #1: chat latency was never the problem).

killers.issue: Anonymous POST /api/welcome/prewarm BEFORE OAuth resolves is an unauthenticated, fingerprint-keyed endpoint that fires real OpenRouter + Fish.audio calls. Cost math is wildly understated: $0.005/warm × 100/day assumes a benign world. Scrapers/competitors finding this endpoint can hit it from rotating IPs for free LLM completions + TTS audio. With Fish.audio S2 priced per character and OpenRouter per-token, a single botnet day at 100k req trivially exceeds $500. The proposed 'IP rate-limit 1/60s' is bypassed by any residential proxy pool. There is currently zero unauthenticated AI surface in the codebase (every /api/chat, /api/companions/templates, /api/tts route uses authenticate middleware except templates/preview which only reads DB).
killers.why: Opens the app's most expensive APIs to anonymous users keyed on a client-supplied fingerprint. Even the proposal's own mitigation ('cap daily anonymous warms') means a single bad actor flips the kill switch and breaks the feature for everyone for the rest of the day — i.e., it self-DOSes.

killers.issue: sessionStorage does NOT survive the Apple OAuth round-trip on native iOS. Apple Sign-In opens a native sheet (ASAuthorizationController) — not a redirect — so sessionStorage technically persists in-WebView. BUT for the Google web fallback path (handleWeb in GoogleSignIn.jsx:66-93), the code redirects via window.location.href to /api/auth/google → Google → /api/auth/google/callback → /my/login?accessToken=... — a full document navigation that does preserve sessionStorage on the same origin, EXCEPT it lands on /my/login (auth-api.js line 616), not /chat/{companionId}. The proposal says auth response 'now includes {companionId, conversationId}' for the API path, but the redirect-based Google flow doesn't return JSON — it redirects with tokens in URL params. The whole 'auth body carries lovetta-welcome-pick' design only works for native paths; the web Google path needs server-side recovery via the existing state base64 blob, which is mentioned but adds another fragile coupling.
killers.why: The pre-warm cache key (fingerprint) and the post-auth companion creation (sessionStorage pick) live on two different channels that don't reliably reconverge across the web Google OAuth redirect. Cache-miss rate will be much higher than 'common case' on web, which is still ~40% of traffic per the weekly analysis.

killers.issue: Forcing the template pick at T-0 directly contradicts Diagnosis A finding #4 cited in the proposal itself: 'custom-companion creators have 51% retention vs 3% for template-pickers among ghosters.' The fix herds 100% of unauth users into the low-retention template path, then bolts on a '4th tile Create your own' as mitigation — but the 4th tile bypasses the pre-warm and thus the entire magic-moment payoff. So you've moved the high-value 19% cohort onto a flow that is strictly worse than today (extra welcome screen, no time-savings benefit), and forced the low-value 81% into a faster version of a flow that already converts at 3%. Math doesn't pencil out: even if the templated 81% improves from 3% → 10%, the custom 19% losing 5pp from added friction (51% → 46%) cancels most of the gain.
killers.why: The proposal optimizes the volume cohort by degrading the value cohort. The retention forecast (+14-21pp) doesn't account for the custom-creator regression at all and assumes the 33-user 'never-created' cohort exists because of friction — but Diagnosis A finding #1 was that they made zero post-signup requests, which means they likely bailed at the OAuth round-trip itself, not at the companion step. Forcing a pick earlier doesn't help users who never finish auth.

killers.issue: Deleting the scene-gen call (companion-api.js:224-238) AND skipping memory/intelligence pre-calls on turn 1 (chat-api.js:560-564) silently degrades message quality. The proposal claims the empty-context branch fires anyway at line 579 for new users, but doesn't verify what buildMemoryContext/buildUserContext/buildSceneContext actually return for a brand-new user — they may be cheap no-ops already, in which case 'skip them' saves zero ms. If they DO populate context that informs the system prompt, you've shipped an A/B test where variant B has a measurably different reply quality on turn 1 vs all subsequent turns and vs control. This poisons the A/B readout (you can't attribute the retention delta to UX vs quality).
killers.why: Confounds the experiment. You can't tell if the +14pp retention came from the magic moment or from a 5% quality regression that happened to be net-positive. And once you ramp to 100%, you can't roll back the deletion without re-introducing latency.

killers.issue: Auto-attaching the template poster as media_url on the SECOND assistant message and labeling it as 'she sent you a photo' is borderline deceptive — the user just saw that same poster as a looping avatar on the welcome screen 30 seconds ago. The proposal's mitigation ('serve a different angle from custom_avatars') still uses pre-baked stock images that all templated users share. This is the exact mechanic that gets AI girlfriend apps roasted in App Store reviews ('all her photos are the same'). Compounds with Apple's evolving 'deceptive UI' enforcement on companion apps.
killers.why: Short-term retention lift, medium-term review-store damage and refund risk. Lovetta is already on a delicate footing with Apple as an 18+ companion app — shipping pre-canned photos labeled as fresh ones is exactly the pattern App Review flags.

killers.issue: Implementation effort is mis-scoped as 'large' but is actually a 2-3 week native + server + Redis + flag-infra job. Touches: 12 modified files + 2 new files + 3 new endpoints + 3 migrations + Redis prewarm cache + Fish.audio R2 streaming + Apple/Google native bridge changes + sessionStorage cross-redirect persistence + iOS audio session unlock + paywall suppression flag + state-param recovery on web Google flow + analytics for is_welcome + A/B gating server-side AND client-side + iOS native rebuild + App Review for behavioral change. Each one is non-trivial. The Apple Sign-In dynamic import requirement alone (per CLAUDE.md memory) means the prewarm fire-and-forget must dynamic-import a separate module before the SignInWithApple call — that ordering matters and the proposal hand-waves it. Realistic ship time before first A/B read: 3-4 weeks.
killers.why: The proposal sells this as a single sprint. Mis-scoping the timeline means it competes against simpler approaches (better first-message prompt, defer paywall only, auto-create companion on signup) that ship in 2-3 days and capture 60-70% of the retention upside without the cost/audio/scraper/A/B-confound risks.

killers.issue: The 'force-play TTS on first message only, then revert to auto_audio=false' mechanism mutates the user's audio preference invisibly. The default in migrate.js v53 is auto_audio=false. The proposal says it uses ?welcome=1 to bypass the saved pref — but ChatPage.jsx line 41-47 reads pref once on mount into autoAudioRef.current. Any code that force-plays must NOT mutate autoAudioRef (or all subsequent messages auto-play, contradicting the default). The proposal acknowledges this but the 'clear after one playback' state machine is fragile: refreshing the page mid-welcome, navigating to Profile and back, or a TTS generation failure (which is common — Fish.audio cold-start can exceed the 20s waitForMessageAudio timeout) all leave the flag in an undefined state.
killers.why: Recoverable bug-class issue, but ties into the autoplay blocker — if the play() rejects, you've still consumed the 'first-play' slot and the user never hears anything.

critiques.refute_one_line: The premise (auto-voice on first load) is blocked by iOS autoplay policy in WKWebView, the anonymous prewarm endpoint is a cost-explosion vector, and the design degrades the high-retention custom-companion cohort to optimize the low-retention template cohort — so even if shipped flawlessly the math doesn't beat a 3-day version that just defers the paywall and fixes the gratitude-monologue prompt.

scores.approach_name: Direct-to-Chat: signup lands inside a conversation with Lily
scores.notes: Medium-effort, low-risk approach that ships in days and has a clean feature flag, but the critic's data shows 0/40 ghosters hit a paywall and 90% never created a companion — so removing Pricing from the cold path fixes a screen most dropouts never reach. Still the safest, most-shippable bet that bundles the high-leverage prompt rewrite.

Ambitious parallel pre-warm + auto-voice concept, but iOS WKWebView autoplay policy blocks the entire 'magic moment' premise and the anonymous prewarm endpoint is a cost-explosion vector. Forces template-pickers (3% retention cohort) while degrading the custom-creator (51% retention) path.

Highest theoretical retention ceiling but schema-impossible FK (INT vs UUID), 117 server touchpoints assuming user_id non-null, UK age-verification law geo-blocks 23% of traffic, and iOS IAP/App Store carve-out kills the most valuable monetization surface. Multi-week refactor disguised as 'large.'

Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) + 1.2 rejection risk on an 18+ NSFW app is existential, recovery via 6-word BIP39 codes is fantasy (crypto wallets see 30-50% loss with money on the line), and synthetic @anon.lovetta.ai emails would poison Resend deliverability. Trades measurable problem for unmeasurable existential risks.

scores.approach_name: Try Before You Sign: Public Live-Chat Demo on the Landing Page
scores.notes: Pre-content age/ToS consent violates UK OSA + Texas HB 1181 + Stripe AUP, paid-ads bid-signal collapses when signup event moves 30-90s later (kills the traffic source feeding 186/186 web signups), and exposes an unauthenticated sexually-flirty LLM endpoint as a CSAM-bait honeypot. Worst risk profile of all five.

ranked_order: Direct-to-Chat: signup lands inside a conversation with Lily
Try Before You Sign: Public Live-Chat Demo on the Landing Page
